36	    warnings.warn(

197	            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
198	            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
199	            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
200	            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]
201

207	                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

814	def try_execute(commands, force_complete = False):
815	    for command in commands:
816	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell = True, stdout = subprocess.PIPE, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT, bufsize = 1) as sp:
817	            for line in sp.stdout:
818	                line = line.decode("utf-8", errors = "replace")
819	                if "undefined reference" in line:
820	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
821	                elif "deprecated" in line:
822	                    return "CMAKE"
823	                elif "Unknown argument" in line:
824	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
825	                elif "***" in line:
826	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
827	                print(line, flush = True, end = "")
828	            pass
829	            if force_complete and sp.returncode is not None and sp.returncode != 0:
830	                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, sp.args)
831	        pass
832	    pass
833	    return None
834	pass

837	def install_llama_cpp_old(version = -10):
838	    # Download the 10th latest release since the latest might be broken!
839	    # FALLBACK mechanism
840	    releases = subprocess.check_output(["git", "ls-remote", "--tags", "https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp.git"])
841	    releases = releases.decode("utf-8").replace("\t", " ").split("\n")
842	    for i, x in enumerate(releases):
843	        if "refs/tags/b" not in x: break
844	    releases = releases[:i]
845	    latest = releases[-1]
846	    version = releases[version].split(" ")[0]
847	
848	    # Check if the llama.cpp exists
849	    if os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
850	        print(
851	            "**[WARNING]** You have a llama.cpp directory which is broken.\n"\
852	            "Unsloth will DELETE the broken directory and install a new one.\n"\
853	            "Press CTRL + C / cancel this if this is wrong. We shall wait 30 seconds.\n"
854	        )
855	        import time
856	        for i in range(30):
857	            print(f"**[WARNING]** Deleting llama.cpp directory... {30-i} seconds left.")
858	            time.sleep(1)
859	        import shutil
860	        shutil.rmtree("llama.cpp", ignore_errors = True)
861	    pass
862	
863	    # Clone a specific commit
864	    # Also don't use the GPU!
865	    commands = [
866	        "git clone --recursive https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp",
867	        f"cd llama.cpp && git reset --hard {version} && git clean -df",
868	    ]
869	    try_execute(commands)
870	
871	    # Try using MAKE
872	    commands = [
873	        "make clean -C llama.cpp",
874	        f"make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
875	    ]
876	    if try_execute(commands) == "CMAKE":
877	        # Instead use CMAKE
878	        commands = [
879	            "cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON",
880	            f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
881	            "cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp",
882	            "rm -rf llama.cpp/build",
883	        ]
884	        try_execute(commands)
885	    pass
886	
887	    # Check if successful
888	    if not (
889	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe") or
890	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize") or
891	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe") or
892	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize") or
893	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize") or
894	        os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize")
895	    ):
896	        raise RuntimeError(
897	            "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
898	            "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
899	            "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()

126	        if self.optimizer is None:

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass

215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass

94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)

268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):

284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype

290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize

293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists

295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]

303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]

310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]

321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]

323	            if out is None:

905	def install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False):
906	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
907	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
908	    # use_cuda = "LLAMA_CUDA=1" if use_cuda else ""
909	
910	    commands = [
911	        "git clone --recursive https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp",
912	        "pip install gguf protobuf",
913	    ]
914	    if os.path.exists("llama.cpp"): return
915	    try_execute(commands)
916	
917	    commands = [
918	        "make clean -C llama.cpp",
919	        # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
920	        # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
921	        # f"{use_cuda} make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
922	        f"make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
923	    ]
924	    if try_execute(commands) == "CMAKE":
925	        # Instead use CMAKE
926	        commands = [
927	            "cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON",
928	            f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
929	            "cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp",
930	            "rm -rf llama.cpp/build",
931	        ]
932	        try_execute(commands)
933	    pass
934	pass

937	def get_executable(executables):
938	    # Get system locations (System Path).split(system separator)
939	    system_directories = os.environ.get("PATH").split(os.pathsep)
940	
941	    for directory in system_directories:
942	        for executable in executables:
943	            path = os.path.join(directory, executable)
944	            # Check if the executable exists and is executable
945	            if os.path.exists(path) and os.access(path, os.X_OK): return path
946	        pass
947	    pass
948	    return None

956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",

962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\

2569	def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
2570	    import inspect
2571	    import types
2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List
2573	
2574	    # And now re add our saving methods!
2575	    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2576	        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
2577	    else:
2578	        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
2579	    pass
2580	
2581	    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
2582	    signature = signature[1:]
2583	    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """
2590	    arguments = dict(locals())
2591	    del arguments["self"]
2592	    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
2593	        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
2594	        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
2597	    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:
2601	        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
2602	        if commit_message is not None:
2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
2605	                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
2606	        else:
2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag
2621	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2622	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
2623	            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
2624	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
2625	            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
2626	        )
2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model
2643	    while True:
2644	
2645	        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
2647	            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
2648	            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
2649	                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2650	            pass
2651	        pass
2652	
2653	        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
2654	        else: break
2655	    pass
2656	
2657	    # Add saving methods to top level model
2658	    if not vision:

2662	            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
2663	            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
2664	            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)

1587	    # Check if BOS added already, then warn
1588	    fix_bos_token = False

1592	        if chat_template is not None and \
1593	            (
1594	                tokenizer.bos_token in chat_template or \
1595	                "{bos_token}" in chat_template.replace(" ", "") or \
1596	                "{bos_token+" in chat_template.replace(" ", "")
1597	            ):
1598	
1599	            fix_bos_token = True
1600	            logger.warning(
1601	                "Unsloth: ##### The current model auto adds a BOS token.\n"\

1618	def create_ollama_modelfile(tokenizer, gguf_location):
1619	    """
1620	        Creates an Ollama Modelfile.
1621	        Use ollama.create(model = "new_ollama_model", modelfile = modelfile)
1622	    """
1623	    modelfile = getattr(tokenizer, "_ollama_modelfile", None)
1624	    if modelfile is None: return None
1625	
1626	    FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER = "⚫@✅#🦥__FILE_LOCATION__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1627	    EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER     = "⚫@✅#🦥__EOS_TOKEN__⚡@🦥#⛵"
1628	    LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER  = "⚫@✅#🦥"
1629	    RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER = "⚡@🦥#⛵"
1630	
1631	    # Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1087
1632	    # We must convert all {'s and }'s but keep {__FILE_LOCATION__} intact
1633	    modelfile = modelfile\
1634	        .replace("{__FILE_LOCATION__}", FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER)\
1635	        .replace("{__EOS_TOKEN__}",     EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER)\
1636	        .replace("{", LEFT_BRACKET_REPLACER)\
1637	        .replace("}", RIGHT_BRACKET_REPLACER)
1638	
1639	    # Revert {__FILE_LOCATION__} back
1640	    modelfile = modelfile\
1641	        .replace(FILE_LOCATION_REPLACER, "{__FILE_LOCATION__}")\
1642	        .replace(EOS_TOKEN_REPLACER,     "{__EOS_TOKEN__}")
1643	
1644	    if "__EOS_TOKEN__" in modelfile:
1645	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1646	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1647	            __EOS_TOKEN__      = tokenizer.eos_token,
1648	        )
1649	    else:
1650	        modelfile = modelfile.format(
1651	            __FILE_LOCATION__  = gguf_location,
1652	        )
1653	    pass
1654	
1655	    modelfile = modelfile\
1656	        .replace("⚫@✅#🦥", "{")\
1657	        .replace("⚡@🦥#⛵", "}")\
1658	        .rstrip()
1659	
1660	    return modelfile
1661	pass

1663	def create_ollama_model(
1664	    username: str,
1665	    model_name: str,
1666	    tag: str,
1667	    modelfile_path: str
1668	):
1669	    try:
1670	        init_check = subprocess.run(
1671	            ['curl', 'http://localhost:11434'], capture_output=True, text=True,  timeout=3
1672	        )
1673	        if init_check.returncode == 0:
1674	            print(init_check.stdout.strip())
1675	        else:
1676	            print("Ollama Server is not Running")
1677	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
1678	        return "Ollama Request Timeout"
1679	
1680	    process = subprocess.Popen(
1681	            ['ollama', 'create', f'{username}/{model_name}:{tag}', '-f', f'{modelfile_path}'],
1682	        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
1683	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
1684	        text=True,
1685	        bufsize=1,
1686	        universal_newlines=True
1687	    )
1688	
1689	    for line in iter(process.stdout.readline, ''):
1690	        print(line, end='')
1691	        sys.stdout.flush()
1692	
1693	    return_code = process.wait()


---

### 2. KEY SAVE/EXPORT METHODS

**Main Save Function:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:204-760`
- **Function:** `unsloth_save_model()`
- **Signature:**
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]

    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags: List[str] = None,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
)
```

**Export Methods (Patched onto Model):**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2569-2678` (patch_saving_functions)
- Methods patched dynamically:
  - `model.push_to_hub_merged()`
  - `model.save_pretrained_merged()`
  - `model.push_to_hub_gguf()`
  - `model.save_pretrained_gguf()`
  - `model.save_pretrained_torchao()`
  - `model.push_to_hub_ggml()`
  - `model.save_pretrained_ggml()`

**Generic Save Methods:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2356-2499`
- `unsloth_generic_save()` - Core save logic
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` - User-facing API
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` - User-facing API

**GGUF Conversion Methods:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:952-1480` (save_to_gguf)
- Converts merged 16bit models to GGUF format with quantization options
- Supports: f32, bf16, f16, q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q2_k, q3_k variants, etc.

**LoRA-Only Push to Hub:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:313-376`
- Special handling for `save_method == "lora"` with push_to_hub
- Uses PEFT's native `push_to_hub()` method

---

### 3. LoRA ADAPTER STORAGE & HANDLING

**LoRA Parameter Extraction Functions:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:190-257`

Function: `get_lora_parameters(proj)` 
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

Function: `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)` - Similar but includes bias

**LoRA Storage Structure (PEFT-Based):**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - LoRA A matrix (Linear layer)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - LoRA B matrix (Linear layer)
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - LoRA scaling factor (usually lora_alpha / rank)
- `proj.base_layer.weight` - Base weight matrix
- `proj.base_layer.bias` - Optional bias

**LoRA Configuration:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py:2265-2617`
- Default LoRA settings:
  - `r = 16` (rank)
  - `lora_alpha = 16` (alpha)
  - `lora_dropout = 0.0`
  - `bias = "none"`
  - `target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`
  - `modules_to_save = None` (can be ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"])
  - `init_lora_weights = True`

**LoRA Merging:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:159-187`
- Function: `_merge_lora(layer, name)`
- Merges via: `W += s * A.T @ B.T` (addmm_ operation)

---

### 4. MODEL CLASSES WITH SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONALITY

**Main Model Loaders:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py:107-526`

Class: `FastLanguageModel`
- Static method: `from_pretrained()` - Load and setup model
- Static method: `get_peft_model()` - Apply LoRA configuration
- Static method: `patch_peft_model()` - Patch with optimized kernels

Class: `FastModel` (line 526)
- Universal model wrapper

Class: `FastVisionModel` (line 995)
- Vision model support

Class: `FastTextModel` (line 998)
- Text model support

**Base Architecture Classes:**
- `FastLlamaModel` - Llama-based models
- `FastMistralModel` - Mistral models
- `FastQwen2Model` - Qwen2 models
- `FastQwen3Model` - Qwen3 models
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` - Qwen3 MoE models
- `FastGemmaModel` - Gemma models
- `FastGemma2Model` - Gemma2 models
- `FastCohereModel` - Cohere models
- `FastGraniteModel` - Granite models
- `FastFalconH1Model` - Falcon H1 models

**Key Method on Models:**
```python
model.get_peft_model(
    r=16,
    target_modules=[...],
    lora_alpha=16,
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    bias="none",
    ...
)
```

Returns: PEFT-wrapped model with LoRA adapters

---

### 5. MAIN ENTRY POINTS / INIT FILES

**Primary Entry Point:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/__init__.py:1-270`
- Imports and exports:
  - All model classes via `from .models import *`
  - Save functions via `from .save import *`
  - Chat templates via `from .chat_templates import *`
  - Tokenizer utils via `from .tokenizer_utils import *`
  - Trainer utilities via `from .trainer import *`
  - Patches TRL trainers via `_patch_trl_trainer()`

**Models Init:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/models/__init__.py:15-29`
- Exports:
  - `FastLanguageModel`, `FastVisionModel`, `FastTextModel`, `FastModel`
  - All architecture-specific models
  - `PatchDPOTrainer`, `PatchKTOTrainer`
  - `is_bfloat16_supported`, `is_vLLM_available`, `__version__`
  - `PatchFastRL`, `vLLMSamplingParams`

**Kernels Init:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py:15-47`
- Core kernel exports:
  - `get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`
  - `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu`, `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact/approx`
  - `apply_lora_qkv`, `apply_lora_o`
  - `fast_lora_forward`
  - `fast_dequantize`, `fast_gemv`, `QUANT_STATE`, `matmul_lora`

---

### 6. LoRA KERNEL OPERATIONS

**LoRA Forward Pass:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py:28-174`
- Class: `LoRA_MLP` (custom autograd Function)
- Implements optimized forward/backward for LoRA merging in SwiGLU MLPs
- Formula: `output = _forward(input + A.T @ B.T * scaling)`

**LoRA Linear Forward:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:691-732`
- Function: `fast_linear_forward(proj, X, temp_lora=None, out=None)`
- Handles 4-bit dequantization + LoRA forward pass

**LoRA Matrix Multiplication:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:734+`
- Function: `matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out=None)`
- Computes: `X @ (W + (A.T @ B.T) * s)`

**LoRA Quantization Support:**
- Handles both 4-bit quantized base weights and LoRA weights
- Dequantizes on-the-fly during forward/backward passes

---

### 7. SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONALITY DETAILS

**Save Methods Flow:**

1. **LoRA-Only Save (Fastest):**
   - `model.save_pretrained(save_directory)` - Standard PEFT save
   - Saves only adapter_config.json and adapter weights
   - ~50MB for 7B model

2. **Merged 16-bit Save:**
   - `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, save_method="merged_16bit")`
   - Merges LoRA into base weights (float16)
   - Suitable for GGUF conversion
   - ~14GB for 7B model

3. **Merged 4-bit Save:**
   - `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, save_method="merged_4bit")`
   - Merges LoRA into 4-bit quantized weights
   - Suitable for DPO/inference with quantization

4. **GGUF Conversion:**
   - `model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, quantization_method="q4_k_m")`
   - Requires merged 16-bit model first
   - Converts via llama.cpp
   - Supports multiple quantization formats

**Push to Hub Flow:**

1. **LoRA Push:**
   ```python
   model.push_to_hub(repo_id="username/model-name")
   ```
   - Calls PEFT's native push_to_hub

2. **Merged Push:**
   ```python
   model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id="username/model-name", save_method="merged_16bit")
   ```
   - Merges locally, then pushes merged weights

3. **GGUF Push:**
   ```python
   model.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id="username/model-name")
   ```
   - Converts to GGUF, then pushes

**HuggingFace Integration:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:1495-1583`
- Function: `upload_to_huggingface()`
- Creates repo, uploads files, adds model cards
- Patches model with "unsloth" tags
- Handles tensorboard event files

---

### 8. KEY CONFIGURATION STRUCTURES

**LoRA Config (PEFT LoraConfig):**
```python
LoraConfig(
    r=16,                                          # Rank
    lora_alpha=16,                                 # Scaling: lora_alpha/r
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", ...],    # Which layers to apply LoRA
    lora_dropout=0.0,                             # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    bias="none",                                  # Bias training
    task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,                # Task type
    layers_to_transform=None,                     # Layer indices
    init_lora_weights=True,                       # Initialize with kaiming_uniform
    loftq_config={},                              # LoftQ quantization config
    use_rslora=False,                             # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save=["lm_head", "embed_tokens"],  # Modules trained fully
)
```

**Save Config:**
```python
unsloth_save_model(
    model, tokenizer,
    save_directory="./model",
    save_method="merged_16bit",      # lora | merged_16bit | merged_4bit
    push_to_hub=False,
    max_shard_size="5GB",
    safe_serialization=True,
    temporary_location="_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage=0.9,
)
```

---

### 9. KEY FILE PATHS

| Functionality | File | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| Main entry | `/unsloth/__init__.py` | 1-270 |
| Save/Export core | `/unsloth/save.py` | 1-2679 |
| Trainer wrapper | `/unsloth/trainer.py` | 1-233 |
| Model loading | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | 1-1000+ |
| LoRA config | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 2265-2617 |
| LoRA patching | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 2731-2948 |
| LoRA kernels | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | 1-400+ |
| LoRA utilities | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 190-500+ |
| Save patching | `/unsloth/save.py` | 2569-2678 |
| LoRA merging | `/unsloth/save.py` | 159-187 |
| GGUF export | `/unsloth/save.py` | 952-1480 |

---

### 10. EXPORT/SAVE FLOW SUMMARY

```
Model Creation:
  FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained() 
    → FastLlamaModel (or variant)
      → get_peft_model() [LoRA setup]
      → patch_peft_model() [Kernel optimization]
      → patch_saving_functions() [Add save methods]

LoRA Storage:
  model.peft_config["default"] = LoraConfig(...)
  For each layer with LoRA:
    layer.lora_A["default"] = Linear(in_dim, rank)
    layer.lora_B["default"] = Linear(rank, out_dim)
    layer.scaling["default"] = lora_alpha / rank

Saving:
  LoRA-Only: model.save_pretrained()
    → PEFT saves adapter_config.json + adapter weights
  
  Merged 16-bit: model.save_pretrained_merged(save_method="merged_16bit")
    → unsloth_save_model() merges W += s*A.T@B.T
    → Saves merged state_dict as float16
  
  GGUF: model.save_pretrained_gguf()
    → First merge to 16-bit
    → Then convert via llama.cpp
    → Apply quantization (q4_k_m, etc)

Push to Hub:
  LoRA: model.push_to_hub(repo_id)
    → PEFT's native push_to_hub()
  
  Merged: model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, save_method="merged_16bit")
    → unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()
    → Merges locally, saves, then pushes
```

---

This comprehensive exploration covers all major components of Unsloth's save/export pipeline, LoRA adapter handling, and model architecture. The codebase is highly modular with clear separation between model loading, LoRA configuration, kernel optimization, and serialization logic.

- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - LoRA A matrix (Linear layer)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - LoRA B matrix (Linear layer)
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - LoRA scaling factor (usually lora_alpha / rank)
- `proj.base_layer.weight` - Base weight matrix
- `proj.base_layer.bias` - Optional bias

**LoRA Configuration:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py:2265-2617`
- Default LoRA settings:
  - `r = 16` (rank)
  - `lora_alpha = 16` (alpha)
  - `lora_dropout = 0.0`
  - `bias = "none"`
  - `target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`
  - `modules_to_save = None` (can be ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"])
  - `init_lora_weights = True`

**LoRA Merging:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:159-187`
- Function: `_merge_lora(layer, name)`
- Merges via: `W += s * A.T @ B.T` (addmm_ operation)

---

### 4. MODEL CLASSES WITH SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONALITY

**Main Model Loaders:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py:107-526`

Class: `FastLanguageModel`
- Static method: `from_pretrained()` - Load and setup model
- Static method: `get_peft_model()` - Apply LoRA configuration
- Static method: `patch_peft_model()` - Patch with optimized kernels

Class: `FastModel` (line 526)
- Universal model wrapper

Class: `FastVisionModel` (line 995)
- Vision model support

Class: `FastTextModel` (line 998)
- Text model support

**Base Architecture Classes:**
- `FastLlamaModel` - Llama-based models
- `FastMistralModel` - Mistral models
- `FastQwen2Model` - Qwen2 models
- `FastQwen3Model` - Qwen3 models
- `FastQwen3MoeModel` - Qwen3 MoE models
- `FastGemmaModel` - Gemma models
- `FastGemma2Model` - Gemma2 models
- `FastCohereModel` - Cohere models
- `FastGraniteModel` - Granite models
- `FastFalconH1Model` - Falcon H1 models

**Key Method on Models:**
```python
model.get_peft_model(
    r=16,
    target_modules=[...],
    lora_alpha=16,
    lora_dropout=0.0,
    bias="none",
    ...
)
```

Returns: PEFT-wrapped model with LoRA adapters

---

### 5. MAIN ENTRY POINTS / INIT FILES

**Primary Entry Point:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/__init__.py:1-270`
- Imports and exports:
  - All model classes via `from .models import *`
  - Save functions via `from .save import *`
  - Chat templates via `from .chat_templates import *`
  - Tokenizer utils via `from .tokenizer_utils import *`
  - Trainer utilities via `from .trainer import *`
  - Patches TRL trainers via `_patch_trl_trainer()`

**Models Init:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/models/__init__.py:15-29`
- Exports:
  - `FastLanguageModel`, `FastVisionModel`, `FastTextModel`, `FastModel`
  - All architecture-specific models
  - `PatchDPOTrainer`, `PatchKTOTrainer`
  - `is_bfloat16_supported`, `is_vLLM_available`, `__version__`
  - `PatchFastRL`, `vLLMSamplingParams`

**Kernels Init:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py:15-47`
- Core kernel exports:
  - `get_lora_parameters`, `get_lora_parameters_bias`
  - `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu`, `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact/approx`
  - `apply_lora_qkv`, `apply_lora_o`
  - `fast_lora_forward`
  - `fast_dequantize`, `fast_gemv`, `QUANT_STATE`, `matmul_lora`

---

### 6. LoRA KERNEL OPERATIONS

**LoRA Forward Pass:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py:28-174`
- Class: `LoRA_MLP` (custom autograd Function)
- Implements optimized forward/backward for LoRA merging in SwiGLU MLPs
- Formula: `output = _forward(input + A.T @ B.T * scaling)`

**LoRA Linear Forward:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:691-732`
- Function: `fast_linear_forward(proj, X, temp_lora=None, out=None)`
- Handles 4-bit dequantization + LoRA forward pass

**LoRA Matrix Multiplication:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py:734+`
- Function: `matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out=None)`
- Computes: `X @ (W + (A.T @ B.T) * s)`

**LoRA Quantization Support:**
- Handles both 4-bit quantized base weights and LoRA weights
- Dequantizes on-the-fly during forward/backward passes

---

### 7. SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONALITY DETAILS

**Save Methods Flow:**

1. **LoRA-Only Save (Fastest):**
   - `model.save_pretrained(save_directory)` - Standard PEFT save
   - Saves only adapter_config.json and adapter weights
   - ~50MB for 7B model

2. **Merged 16-bit Save:**
   - `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, save_method="merged_16bit")`
   - Merges LoRA into base weights (float16)
   - Suitable for GGUF conversion
   - ~14GB for 7B model

3. **Merged 4-bit Save:**
   - `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, save_method="merged_4bit")`
   - Merges LoRA into 4-bit quantized weights
   - Suitable for DPO/inference with quantization

4. **GGUF Conversion:**
   - `model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, quantization_method="q4_k_m")`
   - Requires merged 16-bit model first
   - Converts via llama.cpp
   - Supports multiple quantization formats

**Push to Hub Flow:**

1. **LoRA Push:**
   ```python
   model.push_to_hub(repo_id="username/model-name")
   ```
   - Calls PEFT's native push_to_hub

2. **Merged Push:**
   ```python
   model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id="username/model-name", save_method="merged_16bit")
   ```
   - Merges locally, then pushes merged weights

3. **GGUF Push:**
   ```python
   model.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id="username/model-name")
   ```
   - Converts to GGUF, then pushes

**HuggingFace Integration:**
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:1495-1583`
- Function: `upload_to_huggingface()`
- Creates repo, uploads files, adds model cards
- Patches model with "unsloth" tags
- Handles tensorboard event files

---

### 8. KEY CONFIGURATION STRUCTURES

**LoRA Config (PEFT LoraConfig):**
```python
LoraConfig(
    r=16,                                          # Rank
    lora_alpha=16,                                 # Scaling: lora_alpha/r
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", ...],    # Which layers to apply LoRA
    lora_dropout=0.0,                             # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    bias="none",                                  # Bias training
    task_type=TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,                # Task type
    layers_to_transform=None,                     # Layer indices
    init_lora_weights=True,                       # Initialize with kaiming_uniform
    loftq_config={},                              # LoftQ quantization config
    use_rslora=False,                             # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save=["lm_head", "embed_tokens"],  # Modules trained fully
)
```

**Save Config:**
```python
unsloth_save_model(
    model, tokenizer,
    save_directory="./model",
    save_method="merged_16bit",      # lora | merged_16bit | merged_4bit
    push_to_hub=False,
    max_shard_size="5GB",
    safe_serialization=True,
    temporary_location="_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage=0.9,
)
```

---

### 9. KEY FILE PATHS

| Functionality | File | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| Main entry | `/unsloth/__init__.py` | 1-270 |
| Save/Export core | `/unsloth/save.py` | 1-2679 |
| Trainer wrapper | `/unsloth/trainer.py` | 1-233 |
| Model loading | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | 1-1000+ |
| LoRA config | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 2265-2617 |
| LoRA patching | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 2731-2948 |
| LoRA kernels | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | 1-400+ |
| LoRA utilities | `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | 190-500+ |
| Save patching | `/unsloth/save.py` | 2569-2678 |
| LoRA merging | `/unsloth/save.py` | 159-187 |
| GGUF export | `/unsloth/save.py` | 952-1480 |

---

### 10. EXPORT/SAVE FLOW SUMMARY

```
Model Creation:
  FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained() 
    → FastLlamaModel (or variant)
      → get_peft_model() [LoRA setup]
      → patch_peft_model() [Kernel optimization]
      → patch_saving_functions() [Add save methods]

LoRA Storage:
  model.peft_config["default"] = LoraConfig(...)
  For each layer with LoRA:
    layer.lora_A["default"] = Linear(in_dim, rank)
    layer.lora_B["default"] = Linear(rank, out_dim)
    layer.scaling["default"] = lora_alpha / rank

Saving:
  LoRA-Only: model.save_pretrained()
    → PEFT saves adapter_config.json + adapter weights
  
  Merged 16-bit: model.save_pretrained_merged(save_method="merged_16bit")
    → unsloth_save_model() merges W += s*A.T@B.T
    → Saves merged state_dict as float16
  
  GGUF: model.save_pretrained_gguf()
    → First merge to 16-bit
    → Then convert via llama.cpp
    → Apply quantization (q4_k_m, etc)

Push to Hub:
  LoRA: model.push_to_hub(repo_id)
    → PEFT's native push_to_hub()
  
  Merged: model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, save_method="merged_16bit")
    → unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()
    → Merges locally, saves, then pushes
```

---

This comprehensive exploration covers all major components of Unsloth's save/export pipeline, LoRA adapter handling, and model architecture. The codebase is highly modular with clear separation between model loading, LoRA configuration, kernel optimization, and serialization logic.

    This is a convenience wrapper around :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` that
    is bound directly to the model object by
    :func:`patch_saving_functions`.  It mirrors the ``save_pretrained_*``
    naming convention used by the other Unsloth export methods.

    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer a self-contained ONNX graph is
    written to *output_dir* that computes the adapter delta::

        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)

    A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file with rank, alpha, scaling, feature
    dimensions, and the list of target modules is also written.

    Parameters
    ----------
    output_dir : str
        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
        Created automatically if it does not exist.
    opset_version : int, optional
        ONNX opset version (default ``17``).

    Examples
    --------
    >>> model.save_pretrained_onnx("./lora_onnx_export")
    >>> model.save_pretrained_onnx("./lora_onnx_export", opset_version=18)
    """
    export_lora_to_onnx(self, output_dir=output_dir, opset_version=opset_version)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

2567	    model,

2571	    """
2572	    Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.
2573	
2574	    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer in *model* this function builds a
2575	    small, self-contained ONNX graph that computes the *adapter delta*:
2576	
2577	        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)
2578	
2579	    where
2580	        - ``lora_A`` has shape ``[rank, in_features]``
2581	        - ``lora_B`` has shape ``[out_features, rank]``
2582	        - ``scaling`` equals ``alpha / rank``
2583	
2584	    Each layer is written as a separate ``<module_name>.onnx`` file inside
2585	    *output_dir*.  A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file is also written with
2586	    the rank, alpha, scaling factor, feature dimensions, and the set of
2587	    target modules that were exported.
2588	
2589	    Parameters
2590	    ----------
2591	    model : PeftModel / FastLanguageModel
2592	        The (P)EFT model whose active LoRA adapters should be exported.
2593	    output_dir : str
2594	        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
2595	        Created automatically if it does not exist.
2596	    opset_version : int, optional
2597	        ONNX opset version to use (default ``17``).
2598	
2599	    Returns
2600	    -------
2601	    None
2602	
2603	    Raises
2604	    ------
2605	    ImportError
2606	        If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
2607	
2608	    Examples
2609	    --------

2611	    >>> model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("my-lora-model")
2612	    >>> from unsloth.save import export_lora_to_onnx
2613	    >>> export_lora_to_onnx(model, output_dir="./lora_onnx", opset_version=17)

2623	            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
2624	            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
2625	        )
2626	    pass

2628	    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
2629

2631	        "format"        : "unsloth_lora_onnx",
2632	        "opset_version" : opset_version,
2633	        "adapters"      : [],

2635	    target_modules : set = set()
2636	    exported_count : int = 0

2639	        # Only process PEFT LoRA wrapper layers that carry lora_A / lora_B

2641	            continue
2642	
2643	        # Skip layers where adapters are disabled or already merged into base
2644	        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
2645	            continue
2646	
2647	        # Resolve the active adapter name (supports both old and new PEFT APIs)
2648	        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
2649	        if active is None:
2650	            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
2651	        adapter_name: str = active[0] if isinstance(active, (list, tuple)) else active
2652	
2653	        if adapter_name not in module.lora_A:
2654	            continue  # adapter weights not present for this layer
2655	
2656	        lora_A_weight = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight  # [rank, in_features]
2657	        lora_B_weight = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # [out_features, rank]
2658	        scaling       = float(module.scaling[adapter_name])
2659	
2660	        rank        : int = lora_A_weight.shape[0]
2661	        in_features : int = lora_A_weight.shape[1]
2662	        out_features: int = lora_B_weight.shape[0]
2663	
2664	        # Recover alpha; PEFT stores it in lora_alpha[adapter_name]
2665	        lora_alpha_map = getattr(module, "lora_alpha", {})
2666	        alpha = float(lora_alpha_map.get(adapter_name, scaling * rank))
2667

2669	        A_T = lora_A_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [in_features, rank]
2670	        B_T = lora_B_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [rank, out_features]
2671	
2672	        # ------------------------------------------------------------------
2673	        # Build the ONNX graph:
2674	        #
2675	        #   input  (float32, [batch?, seq?, in_features])

2684	        )

2687	        )
2688	
2689	        init_A_T = numpy_helper.from_array(
2690	            A_T.astype(np.float32), name="lora_A_T"
2691	        )
2692	        init_B_T = numpy_helper.from_array(
2693	            B_T.astype(np.float32), name="lora_B_T"
2694	        )
2695	        init_scale = numpy_helper.from_array(
2696	            np.array([scaling], dtype=np.float32), name="scaling"
2697	        )
2698	
2699	        node_matmul_a = onnx_helper.make_node(
2700	            "MatMul",
2701	            inputs  = ["input", "lora_A_T"],
2702	            outputs = ["h"],
2703	            name    = "MatMul_lora_A",
2704	        )
2705	        node_matmul_b = onnx_helper.make_node(
2706	            "MatMul",
2707	            inputs  = ["h", "lora_B_T"],
2708	            outputs = ["lora_delta_unscaled"],
2709	            name    = "MatMul_lora_B",
2710	        )
2711	        node_scale = onnx_helper.make_node(
2712	            "Mul",
2713	            inputs  = ["lora_delta_unscaled", "scaling"],
2714	            outputs = ["lora_delta"],
2715	            name    = "Mul_scaling",
2716	        )
2717	
2718	        graph = onnx_helper.make_graph(
2719	            nodes       = [node_matmul_a, node_matmul_b, node_scale],
2720	            name        = graph_name,
2721	            inputs      = [x_input],
2722	            outputs     = [delta_output],
2723	            initializer = [init_A_T, init_B_T, init_scale],
2724	        )
2725	
2726	        model_proto = onnx_helper.make_model(
2727	            graph,
2728	            opset_imports = [onnx_helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
2729	        )
2730	        model_proto.doc_string = (
2731	            "Unsloth LoRA adapter delta for layer '{}' "
2732	            "(rank={}, alpha={:.4g}, scaling={:.6g})".format(
2733	                module_name, rank, alpha, scaling

@torch.inference_mode
def export_lora_to_onnx(
    model,
    output_dir    : str,
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> None:
    """
    Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer in *model* this function builds a
    small, self-contained ONNX graph that computes the *adapter delta*:

        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)

    where
        - ``lora_A`` has shape ``[rank, in_features]``
        - ``lora_B`` has shape ``[out_features, rank]``
        - ``scaling`` equals ``alpha / rank``

    Each layer is written as a separate ``<module_name>.onnx`` file inside
    *output_dir*.  A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file is also written with
    the rank, alpha, scaling factor, feature dimensions, and the set of
    target modules that were exported.

    Parameters
    ----------
    model : PeftModel / FastLanguageModel
        The (P)EFT model whose active LoRA adapters should be exported.
    output_dir : str
        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
        Created automatically if it does not exist.
    opset_version : int, optional
        ONNX opset version to use (default ``17``).

    Returns
    -------
    None

    Raises
    ------
    ImportError
        If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.

    Examples
    --------
    >>> from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
    >>> model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("my-lora-model")
    >>> from unsloth.save import export_lora_to_onnx
    >>> export_lora_to_onnx(model, output_dir="./lora_onnx", opset_version=17)
    """
    import json
    import numpy as np

    try:
        import onnx
        from onnx import numpy_helper, TensorProto, helper as onnx_helper
    except ImportError:
        raise ImportError(
            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
        )
    pass

    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

    metadata : dict = {
        "format"        : "unsloth_lora_onnx",
        "opset_version" : opset_version,
        "adapters"      : [],
    }
    target_modules : set = set()
    exported_count : int = 0

    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
        # Only process PEFT LoRA wrapper layers that carry lora_A / lora_B
        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
            continue

        # Skip layers where adapters are disabled or already merged into base
        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
            continue

        # Resolve the active adapter name (supports both old and new PEFT APIs)
        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
        if active is None:
            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
        adapter_name: str = active[0] if isinstance(active, (list, tuple)) else active

        if adapter_name not in module.lora_A:
            continue  # adapter weights not present for this layer

        lora_A_weight = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight  # [rank, in_features]
        lora_B_weight = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # [out_features, rank]
        scaling       = float(module.scaling[adapter_name])

        rank        : int = lora_A_weight.shape[0]
        in_features : int = lora_A_weight.shape[1]
        out_features: int = lora_B_weight.shape[0]

        # Recover alpha; PEFT stores it in lora_alpha[adapter_name]
        lora_alpha_map = getattr(module, "lora_alpha", {})
        alpha = float(lora_alpha_map.get(adapter_name, scaling * rank))

        # Convert to float32 numpy arrays for ONNX
        A_T = lora_A_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [in_features, rank]
        B_T = lora_B_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [rank, out_features]

        # ------------------------------------------------------------------
        # Build the ONNX graph:
        #
        #   input  (float32, [batch?, seq?, in_features])
        #     └─► MatMul(lora_A_T)  → h  [batch?, seq?, rank]
        #           └─► MatMul(lora_B_T) → lora_delta_unscaled  [batch?, seq?, out_features]
        #                 └─► Mul(scaling) → lora_delta  [batch?, seq?, out_features]
        # ------------------------------------------------------------------
        graph_name = "lora_{}".format(module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_"))

        x_input = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info(
            "input", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, in_features]
        )
        delta_output = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info(
            "lora_delta", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, out_features]
        )

        init_A_T = numpy_helper.from_array(
            A_T.astype(np.float32), name="lora_A_T"
        )
        init_B_T = numpy_helper.from_array(
            B_T.astype(np.float32), name="lora_B_T"
        )
        init_scale = numpy_helper.from_array(
            np.array([scaling], dtype=np.float32), name="scaling"
        )

        node_matmul_a = onnx_helper.make_node(
            "MatMul",
            inputs  = ["input", "lora_A_T"],
            outputs = ["h"],
            name    = "MatMul_lora_A",
        )
        node_matmul_b = onnx_helper.make_node(
            "MatMul",
            inputs  = ["h", "lora_B_T"],
            outputs = ["lora_delta_unscaled"],
            name    = "MatMul_lora_B",
        )
        node_scale = onnx_helper.make_node(
            "Mul",
            inputs  = ["lora_delta_unscaled", "scaling"],
            outputs = ["lora_delta"],
            name    = "Mul_scaling",
        )

        graph = onnx_helper.make_graph(
            nodes       = [node_matmul_a, node_matmul_b, node_scale],
            name        = graph_name,
            inputs      = [x_input],
            outputs     = [delta_output],
            initializer = [init_A_T, init_B_T, init_scale],
        )

        model_proto = onnx_helper.make_model(
            graph,
            opset_imports = [onnx_helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
        )
        model_proto.doc_string = (
            "Unsloth LoRA adapter delta for layer '{}' "
            "(rank={}, alpha={:.4g}, scaling={:.6g})".format(
                module_name, rank, alpha, scaling
            )
        )
        # IR version 8 is required for opset >= 15
        model_proto.ir_version = 8

        onnx.checker.check_model(model_proto)

        safe_name  = module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_")
        onnx_file  = "{}.onnx".format(safe_name)
        onnx_path  = os.path.join(output_dir, onnx_file)
        onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)

        # Track which weight names were adapted (e.g. "q_proj", "v_proj")
        target_modules.add(module_name.split(".")[-1])

        metadata["adapters"].append({
            "module_name" : module_name,
            "onnx_file"   : onnx_file,
            "adapter_name": adapter_name,
            "rank"        : rank,
            "alpha"       : alpha,
            "scaling"     : scaling,
            "in_features" : in_features,
            "out_features": out_features,
        })
        exported_count += 1
    pass  # end of named_modules loop

    if exported_count == 0:
        import warnings
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers were found in the model. "
            "Nothing was exported."
        )
    pass

    metadata["target_modules"] = sorted(target_modules)

    meta_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_onnx_metadata.json")
    with open(meta_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
        json.dump(metadata, fh, indent=2)

    print(
        "Unsloth: Exported {} LoRA adapter(s) to ONNX format in '{}'.".format(
            exported_count, output_dir
        )
    )
    if target_modules:
        print("         Target modules : {}".format(sorted(target_modules)))
    print("         Metadata saved : {}".format(meta_path))
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
    self,
    output_dir    : str,
    opset_version : int = 17,
) -> None:
    """
    Export this model's LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.

    This is a convenience wrapper around :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` that
    is bound directly to the model object by
    :func:`patch_saving_functions`.  It mirrors the ``save_pretrained_*``
    naming convention used by the other Unsloth export methods.

+@torch.inference_mode
+def export_lora_to_onnx(
+    model,
+    output_dir    : str,
+    opset_version : int = 17,
+) -> None:
+    """
+    Export LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.
+
+    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer in *model* this function builds a
+    small, self-contained ONNX graph that computes the *adapter delta*:
+
+        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)
+
+    where
+        - ``lora_A`` has shape ``[rank, in_features]``
+        - ``lora_B`` has shape ``[out_features, rank]``
+        - ``scaling`` equals ``alpha / rank``
+
+    Each layer is written as a separate ``<module_name>.onnx`` file inside
+    *output_dir*.  A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file is also written with
+    the rank, alpha, scaling factor, feature dimensions, and the set of
+    target modules that were exported.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    model : PeftModel / FastLanguageModel
+        The (P)EFT model whose active LoRA adapters should be exported.
+    output_dir : str
+        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
+        Created automatically if it does not exist.
+    opset_version : int, optional
+        ONNX opset version to use (default ``17``).
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    None
+
+    Raises
+    ------
+    ImportError
+        If the ``onnx`` package is not installed.
+
+    Examples
+    --------
+    >>> from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
+    >>> model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained("my-lora-model")
+    >>> from unsloth.save import export_lora_to_onnx
+    >>> export_lora_to_onnx(model, output_dir="./lora_onnx", opset_version=17)
+    """
+    import json
+    import numpy as np
+
+    try:
+        import onnx
+        from onnx import numpy_helper, TensorProto, helper as onnx_helper
+    except ImportError:
+        raise ImportError(
+            "Unsloth: The `onnx` package is required for ONNX export.\n"
+            "Install it with:  pip install onnx"
+        )
+    pass
+
+    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
+
+    metadata : dict = {
+        "format"        : "unsloth_lora_onnx",
+        "opset_version" : opset_version,
+        "adapters"      : [],
+    }
+    target_modules : set = set()
+    exported_count : int = 0
+
+    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
+        # Only process PEFT LoRA wrapper layers that carry lora_A / lora_B
+        if not isinstance(module, (Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
+            continue
+
+        # Skip layers where adapters are disabled or already merged into base
+        if getattr(module, "disable_adapters", False) or getattr(module, "merged", False):
+            continue
+
+        # Resolve the active adapter name (supports both old and new PEFT APIs)
+        active = getattr(module, "active_adapters", None)
+        if active is None:
+            active = getattr(module, "active_adapter", ("default",))
+        adapter_name: str = active[0] if isinstance(active, (list, tuple)) else active
+
+        if adapter_name not in module.lora_A:
+            continue  # adapter weights not present for this layer
+
+        lora_A_weight = module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight  # [rank, in_features]
+        lora_B_weight = module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight  # [out_features, rank]
+        scaling       = float(module.scaling[adapter_name])
+
+        rank        : int = lora_A_weight.shape[0]
+        in_features : int = lora_A_weight.shape[1]
+        out_features: int = lora_B_weight.shape[0]
+
+        # Recover alpha; PEFT stores it in lora_alpha[adapter_name]
+        lora_alpha_map = getattr(module, "lora_alpha", {})
+        alpha = float(lora_alpha_map.get(adapter_name, scaling * rank))
+
+        # Convert to float32 numpy arrays for ONNX
+        A_T = lora_A_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [in_features, rank]
+        B_T = lora_B_weight.detach().float().cpu().numpy().T  # [rank, out_features]
+
+        # ------------------------------------------------------------------
+        # Build the ONNX graph:
+        #
+        #   input  (float32, [batch?, seq?, in_features])
+        #     └─► MatMul(lora_A_T)  → h  [batch?, seq?, rank]
+        #           └─► MatMul(lora_B_T) → lora_delta_unscaled  [batch?, seq?, out_features]
+        #                 └─► Mul(scaling) → lora_delta  [batch?, seq?, out_features]
+        # ------------------------------------------------------------------
+        graph_name = "lora_{}".format(module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_"))
+
+        x_input = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info(
+            "input", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, in_features]
+        )
+        delta_output = onnx_helper.make_tensor_value_info(
+            "lora_delta", TensorProto.FLOAT, [None, None, out_features]
+        )
+
+        init_A_T = numpy_helper.from_array(
+            A_T.astype(np.float32), name="lora_A_T"
+        )
+        init_B_T = numpy_helper.from_array(
+            B_T.astype(np.float32), name="lora_B_T"
+        )
+        init_scale = numpy_helper.from_array(
+            np.array([scaling], dtype=np.float32), name="scaling"
+        )
+
+        node_matmul_a = onnx_helper.make_node(
+            "MatMul",
+            inputs  = ["input", "lora_A_T"],
+            outputs = ["h"],
+            name    = "MatMul_lora_A",
+        )
+        node_matmul_b = onnx_helper.make_node(
+            "MatMul",
+            inputs  = ["h", "lora_B_T"],
+            outputs = ["lora_delta_unscaled"],
+            name    = "MatMul_lora_B",
+        )
+        node_scale = onnx_helper.make_node(
+            "Mul",
+            inputs  = ["lora_delta_unscaled", "scaling"],
+            outputs = ["lora_delta"],
+            name    = "Mul_scaling",
+        )
+
+        graph = onnx_helper.make_graph(
+            nodes       = [node_matmul_a, node_matmul_b, node_scale],
+            name        = graph_name,
+            inputs      = [x_input],
+            outputs     = [delta_output],
+            initializer = [init_A_T, init_B_T, init_scale],
+        )
+
+        model_proto = onnx_helper.make_model(
+            graph,
+            opset_imports = [onnx_helper.make_opsetid("", opset_version)],
+        )
+        model_proto.doc_string = (
+            "Unsloth LoRA adapter delta for layer '{}' "
+            "(rank={}, alpha={:.4g}, scaling={:.6g})".format(
+                module_name, rank, alpha, scaling
+            )
+        )
+        # IR version 8 is required for opset >= 15
+        model_proto.ir_version = 8
+
+        onnx.checker.check_model(model_proto)
+
+        safe_name  = module_name.replace(".", "_").replace("/", "_")
+        onnx_file  = "{}.onnx".format(safe_name)
+        onnx_path  = os.path.join(output_dir, onnx_file)
+        onnx.save(model_proto, onnx_path)
+
+        # Track which weight names were adapted (e.g. "q_proj", "v_proj")
+        target_modules.add(module_name.split(".")[-1])
+
+        metadata["adapters"].append({
+            "module_name" : module_name,
+            "onnx_file"   : onnx_file,
+            "adapter_name": adapter_name,
+            "rank"        : rank,
+            "alpha"       : alpha,
+            "scaling"     : scaling,
+            "in_features" : in_features,
+            "out_features": out_features,
+        })
+        exported_count += 1
+    pass  # end of named_modules loop
+
+    if exported_count == 0:
+        import warnings
+        warnings.warn(
+            "Unsloth: No active LoRA adapter layers were found in the model. "
+            "Nothing was exported."
+        )
+    pass
+
+    metadata["target_modules"] = sorted(target_modules)
+
+    meta_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "lora_onnx_metadata.json")
+    with open(meta_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
+        json.dump(metadata, fh, indent=2)
+
+    print(
+        "Unsloth: Exported {} LoRA adapter(s) to ONNX format in '{}'.".format(
+            exported_count, output_dir
+        )
+    )
+    if target_modules:
+        print("         Target modules : {}".format(sorted(target_modules)))
+    print("         Metadata saved : {}".format(meta_path))
+pass
+
+
+def unsloth_save_pretrained_onnx(
+    self,
+    output_dir    : str,
+    opset_version : int = 17,
+) -> None:
+    """
+    Export this model's LoRA adapter weights to ONNX format.
+
+    This is a convenience wrapper around :func:`export_lora_to_onnx` that
+    is bound directly to the model object by
+    :func:`patch_saving_functions`.  It mirrors the ``save_pretrained_*``
+    naming convention used by the other Unsloth export methods.
+
+    For every LoRA-augmented linear layer a self-contained ONNX graph is
+    written to *output_dir* that computes the adapter delta::
+
+        lora_delta = scaling * ((x @ lora_A.T) @ lora_B.T)
+
+    A ``lora_onnx_metadata.json`` file with rank, alpha, scaling, feature
+    dimensions, and the list of target modules is also written.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    output_dir : str
+        Directory to write ``.onnx`` files and metadata into.
+        Created automatically if it does not exist.
+    opset_version : int, optional
+        ONNX opset version (default ``17``).
+
+    Examples
+    --------
+    >>> model.save_pretrained_onnx("./lora_onnx_export")
+    >>> model.save_pretrained_onnx("./lora_onnx_export", opset_version=18)
+    """
+    export_lora_to_onnx(self, output_dir=output_dir, opset_version=opset_version)

+pass
